summary: The implementation satisfies the root-reset-defaults task in substance. Reset is gated by root password plus exact confirmation, failures record non-secret audit evidence without changing endpoint state, successful reset deletes current endpoint data and recreates deterministic seed defaults, the UI exposes the confirmation flow, and @root-reset covers API failure plus UI recovery/audit behavior. The task maps directly to docs/product-specs/operator-configuration.md, and the provided deterministic summary shows all required commands passed, including full verify.
satisfied_exit_criteria: Root password and exact confirmation text are required by the reset service/API before mutation.
Failed reset attempts write non-secret system.reset audit records and leave endpoint data intact.
Successful reset clears endpoint, endpoint parameter, and response case data, reseeds the default echo endpoint, and retains audit history.
The /reset UI clearly describes destructive behavior and submits root password plus RESET DEFAULTS confirmation.
tests/e2e/root-reset.spec.ts is tagged @root-reset and proves API failure, UI failure, successful recovery to seed defaults, and audit evidence without leaked submitted passwords.
Required gates are reported passing: npm run db:prepare, npm run test:unit, npm run test:e2e -- --grep @root-reset, and npm run verify.
